title: The Markarian Chain on the Inner Rim
explanation: Across the heart of the Virgo Cluster of Galaxies lies a stunning string of galaxies known as Markarian's Chain. The chain, pictured above from the Hubble Space Telescope and now showing Messier objects M84, M86, NGC 4477, NGC 4478, NGC 4458, NGC 4467, M49, and NGC 4461, is curved because each member galaxy moves differently relative to our line of sight. The massive Virgo Cluster itself is the nearest major cluster of galaxies, and these galaxies are all gravitationally bound together.

title: Crescent Nebula Regenerated
explanation: This beautiful cosmic portrait is the result of a star that died quietly. The Crescent Nebula, also known as NGC 6888, is shown in false-color X-ray and optical image composite. The nebula is formed by the fast stellar wind from the hot star at its center colliding with and energizing the slower wind from the same star that was ejected when it was a red giant star millions of years ago.
